notes: Lesson 21 Orientation (act 122), 'Backtracking — walk the decision tree', 844.1s, 7 long cues (56-148s each). Big-picture what/why. Each long cue builds progressively across several beats (wait_until spacing) and settles on one comprehensive held final frame (3b1b style), so the 'mid' frame legitimately captures an intermediate beat and the settled composition is the 'end'/final frame. Cue0 variable-loop-count motivation (pairs/triples/subsets nested loops -> one recursive walk). Cue1 subsets decision tree, DFS visits every candidate, one root-to-leaf path = one answer. Cue2 three-beats template (Choose/Explore/Un-choose) on a shared path + small tree. Cue3 reference-BUG vs COPY two-panel (saved references all collapse to [] after pops, vs distinct snapshots). Cue4 prune: full {1,2,3} permutation tree, EARLY big red cut near root vs LATE amber leaf cut. Cue5 subsets start-index vs permutations used[] two-column. Cue6 O(2^n)/O(n!) + DP-folds-to-a-table. Uses backtracking.py idiom lib; MathTex only for complexity bounds. Two iterations: authored + manim -s smoke all 7 (fixed Cue03 right-panel label safe-area overshoot -> narrowed to 5.4 width; tightened Cue05 side notes), then full -ql render 844.001s vs audio 844.06s + READ start/mid/end all 7 cues. Per-cue checklist (message matches narration, tree/list correctness, highlight/prune semantics, safe-area via theme.guard, chunk timing pinned): all pass.
